raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "About 5.7 million Californians typically worked in frontline industries, representing about 30 percent of all workers.",
    "Millions of Californians are facing alarmingly unsafe working conditions during the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "The safety of California’s workers, especially those on the frontlines, must be a top priority.",
    "Strong workplace health and safety protections are necessary to curb the spread of COVID-19.",
    "Frontline workers regularly interact with the public or work in close proximity with their colleagues, facing heightened risks due to COVID-19.",
    "Fast-food workers are still denied paid sick days and face outbreaks of the virus on the job.",
    "Cal/OSHA found that some employers, such as Farmer John, are not meeting state standards for worker safety.",
    "Cal/OSHA has issued $1 million in COVID-19 related citations based on nearly 8,000 complaints regarding workplace safety."
  ]
}
```
